creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to ykla from comment #4)

I am not a maintainer of sysutils/rpi-firmware or of any
FreeBSD port or of any part of FreeBSD. My contributions
to the code bases are rare.

My display context works for both hdmi_safe=1 and hdmi_safe=0 .
hdmi_safe=0 is much nicer for my context (more, smaller text,
that my eyesight allows for using, vs. bigger text where not
much fits on screen).

Unfortunately, there are still other types of contexts for
which hdmi_safe=0 does not work but hdmi_safe=1 does work.

In other words: no default configuration always works for all
contexts. The default configuration is set to what the RPi*
documentation indicated as compatible with more contexts,
even if not always as nice for many folks. More folks should
have an initial display to work with as is. It is harder to
not have an initial display and then getting it going so the
attempt is to minimize the count of such based on what the
RPi* documentation reported.

There are type of early-boot problems where an HDMI display
is less effective than a serial console, especially for
gathering and reporting message text: more characters than
fit on screen. Glad you figured out that your hardware
context needs you to avoid hdmi_safe=1 and did not need
the serial port to figure that out.

As you found how to make things work for your display hardware,
you can probably close this bugzilla report and other related
ones that hdmi_safe=0 fixes for you.
